I'll analyze the contract carefully:

1. Canonicalization sets width to `max(4, NumDigits(value))` — padding to minimum 4 digits, but never reducing below what's needed to write the value
2. Kind and value never change
3. Already-canonical ids are unchanged (idempotent)

**(a) Implementation:**

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}
```

Wait — re-reading: "An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged" and "the numeric value is left-zero-padded to a minimum of 4 digits." So if width >= 4, return unchanged. If width < 4, pad to 4.

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}
```

**(b) Lemma with ensures clauses:**

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= x.width
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

Let me verify each clause:
- Kind preserved ✓
- Value preserved ✓  
- Width ≥ 4 (minimum 4 digits) ✓
- Width ≥ original width (never reduces display width... actually wait: "An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged" means if width ≥ 4, keep it; otherwise set to 4. So width only increases or stays same when < 4, stays same when ≥ 4 — so `width >= x.width` only holds when x.width < 4 → result is 4 ≥ x.width. When x.width ≥ 4, result width = x.width. So yes `Canonicalize(x).width >= x.width` holds in all cases) ✓
- Exact characterization of the new width ✓
- Result is wellformed (width can hold value) ✓
- Idempotent: canonicalizing again is a no-op ✓

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= x.width
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

And the implementation:

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}
```
